english: equal to 24 of our seconds, = 6 dbugs (dbugs) Wdk., cf. ču-tsód (chu tshod). — ču-srín (chu srin) a water- or sea-monster, also Capricorn in the Zodiac. — ču-lhá (chu lha) water-god (Varuṇa); also = klui rgyál-po (klu'i rgyal po).
= la-čú (la chu), rhubarb, ču-rtsá (chu rtsa) its root, used as dye and as a laxative C.
four years ago C., W.
1. C. a man's dress, coat, — 2. water-carrier.

a large sinew, of which there are 16 acc. to Tibetan anatomy; ču-ba ldóg-pa (chu ba ldog pa) a contraction of the sinews Cs., žá-ba (zha ba) lameness, paralysis of the sinews Sch. — ču-rgyús (chu rgyus) (स्नायु) sinews, ligaments and nerves (there are 900 rgyús-pa (rgyus pa)); with respect to these, as well as to the veins, Tibetan science seems to be rather in the dark, — ču-rtsá (chu rtsa) 'sinew-veins', a term coming nearest to what we call the nerves. — ču-ba-lṅa-ldán (chu ba lnga ldan), and lṅa-lén (lnga len) Cs. names of countries in India.
a medicinal herb Med.
*ṭu-gu ču-ma-lóṅ* (tu gu chu ma long) Ld. an infant, baby.
the external and internal urinary organs.
v. _júg-pa ('jug pa).

1. adj. col. čuṅ-ṅu (chung ngu), W. also *čuṅ-se* (chung se), little, small, čuṅ-čés-pas (chung ches pas) Dzl. when he was very little; young, bu čuṅ-ba (bu chung ba) or -ṅu (-ngu) the younger or the youngest son; lo-čuṅ (lo chung) young in years; ma-čuṅ (ma chung) the younger sister of the mother; *á-pa čuṅ-ṅu* (a pa chung ngu) the younger brother of the father; the younger or the youngest of the fathers (in polyandry); čuṅ-ṅu-nas (chung ngu nas) up from infancy; čuṅ-grógs (chung grogs) an early friend, friend of one's youth; čuṅ-zád (chung zad) a little, cf. čuṅ-zad (chung zad); čuṅ- (gyi) sri (chung gyi sri) a devil devouring infants, infants-devil; *ñiṅ-kám-* (nying kam), *ño-míg-* (nyo mig), *pód-* (pod), or *ñom-čuṅ-se* (nyom chung se) W. shy, timid. — 2. vb. to be little, small etc., sñiṅ ma čuṅ čig (snying ma chung cig) Glr., be not timorous, do not fear! _dún-ma rgya ma čuṅ čig ('dun ma rgya ma chung cig) let the consultation not be tri-fling, let at once something of moment be consulted; _dún-ma rgya mi čuṅ-bar byed-do ('dun ma rgya mi chung bar byed do) let us now decide on important things Glr.; čuṅs-pa (chungs pa) pf., čuṅs-pa yin-nam (chungs pa yin nam) is it too unimportant? Mil. (čuṅ-ʼjúg (chung 'jug) v. ja (ja)).

C. also *čuṅ-grógs* (chung grogs), *čuṅ-_drís* (chung 'dris), resp. btsún-mo (btsun mo), wife, consort, partner B., C.; lén-pa (len pa) to marry; mi žíg-gi čuṅ-mar byéd-pa (mi zhig gi chung mar byed pa) to be made a man's wife, to be married.

occurs only in čud-yzón-pa (chud gzon pa), -ysón-pa (-gson pa), -ysán-pa (-gsan pa), -zá-ba (-za ba), seldom -_dzá-ba (-'dza' ba) (Lex.) to consume, spend, waste; čud mi zá-ba (chud mi za ba) inexhaustible.

= _tsúd-pa ('tsud pa), _júg-pa ('jug pa), to go, get in or into, to enter, to put in or into etc., to go into a town Dzl.; of food entering the body Dzl.; gaṅ yaṅ rúṅ-ba mi čúd-pa med (gang yang rung ba mi chud pa med) all without distinction may enter (my religion), says Buddha, in opposition to the aristocratic exclusiveness of Brahmanism Dzl.; t́úgs-su čúd-pa (thugs su chud pa), resp., to impress on one's mind; koṅ-du (kong du) v. koṅ-pa (kong pa); _óg-tu ('og tu) to subject Tar.; grábs-su (grabs su) c. accus. to procure, to provide or furnish one's self with a thing Mil.
occurs in žiṅ-čun (zhing chun) one that is watering or taking care of fields, tsás-čun (tsas chun) gardens, _ól-čun ('ol chun) meadows Ld.
1. bunch, bundle. 2. tuft, tassel, ornament, of silk, pearls etc.
the second wife in rank.
Bal. little, small.

a corruption of čúd-pa (chud pa), kun čúb-par byao (kun chub par bya'o) all this is to be well impressed on the mind; dbaṅ-po t́ams-čád-du čúb-pa (dbang po thams cad du chub pa) to pass through, to penetrate, every organ Stg.
_jígs-čum-pa ('jigs chum pa) Mil., to shrink, to crouch with fear.
termin. of ču (chu); čur _čí-ba (chur 'chi ba) to be drowned.
a kind of vermicelli, prepared from butter-milk boiled Med., Ld. *čúrpe* (chur pe).
